mergeRationale: The refactor removes the per-hop request chaining, so method/body changes made by one redirect hop (e.g. a 303) are silently discarded on the next hop.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch keeps using the original request as the base for every redirect hop.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: After a 303 changes POST to GET, a later 307 should preserve GET, not revive the original POST (and its body).
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Keep updating the current request after each hop (req = prepared_request) and use that as the base for the next redirect.

requiredFindings.summary: The new test does not model POST -> 303 -> GET -> 307; it asserts something that was already true before the patch.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Rebinding a local variable never mutated the caller's request, so the new assertion passes on the old code too. Single-hop behavior can pass while the multi-hop chain is wrong.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add a redirect-chain test with 303 followed by 307 and assert the final method is GET.

optionalFindings.summary: The side effect the PR claims to remove does not exist: rebinding the local name req never mutated the caller's request object.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because the added regression test passes.
Copying from the original request on every hop is safe because PreparedRequest.copy() is a deep copy.
